title: Add tests and verification plan for macOS device owner authentication
summary: Define and implement automated and manual verification coverage for the macOS authenticated secret reveal flow, including CLI behavior, Python backend integration, helper failure mapping, and host-level authentication checks.
user_intent: Ensure the new macOS device owner authentication flow is covered by tests and has a reproducible verification procedure before rollout.
outputs: Automated tests for authenticated reveal CLI behavior
Backend tests for helper invocation and error handling
Mocked integration tests for success and failure paths
Manual verification checklist for Touch ID / Apple Watch-capable macOS hosts
Updated verification notes in docs if required
acceptance_criteria: There are automated tests covering the authenticated plaintext reveal command path.
There are tests for unsupported platform handling and missing-helper failure behavior.
There are tests confirming authentication cancellation and helper execution failures do not fall back to plaintext output.
There are tests for legacy plaintext flags if they remain supported during migration.
A manual verification checklist exists for macOS host testing, including Touch ID success, cancel flow, unavailable-auth flow, and Apple Watch-assisted approval when the host supports it.
Verification guidance clearly distinguishes unit-testable behavior from host-only authentication behavior.
implementation_notes: Prefer unit tests around command routing and backend abstraction boundaries instead of trying to perform real biometric authentication in automated test runs.
Mock the native helper process boundary so stdout, stderr, exit codes, and missing executable behavior can be validated deterministically.
Assert that error messages are sanitized and never contain secret material.
Document host-only verification steps separately from automated tests to keep CI stable.

unit_tests: secret reveal routes through the authenticated retrieval path on macOS
secret reveal fails closed on unsupported platforms
secret reveal fails when the secret ref is missing or invalid
legacy plaintext flags route through the authenticated path if still supported
no code path prints plaintext after auth cancellation or helper failure
backend_tests: helper command arguments are constructed correctly from service/account
successful helper stdout is returned as the secret value
non-zero helper exit codes are mapped to stable user-facing errors
missing helper executable is reported as a closed failure
stderr handling does not leak sensitive data
integration_tests: mocked keychain item not found response
manual_verification: Touch ID success path on a supported Mac
authentication cancel path returns failure without plaintext output
authentication unavailable path returns a clear error
Apple Watch-assisted approval works when macOS offers it
unauthenticated TTY-based plaintext reveal is blocked

risks: Automated tests may overfit to helper error strings if the mapping contract is not defined clearly.
Host authentication behavior varies by hardware and macOS configuration, so manual verification must be explicit.
Legacy compatibility flags may create duplicate test surfaces during the migration period.

automated: Run project test suite covering CLI and backend modules
Confirm new tests pass consistently in non-macOS and macOS CI where applicable
manual: Run authenticated reveal on a Touch ID-capable Mac
Repeat on a host configured for Apple Watch approval if available
Capture concise verification notes for success and failure cases

definition_of_done: Automated tests for authenticated reveal behavior are merged.
A manual macOS verification checklist is written and usable.
Failure modes are covered without leaking secret values.
The verification procedure is sufficient to support rollout of the authenticated reveal feature.
